title.en: No, you're overpraising me! 아니에요, 과찬이십니다! (Workplace Praise)

description.en: Season 2 Situation Masterclass begins! When you receive praise from a boss in a Korean company, practice reacting politely and humbly.
description.ko: 시즌 2 상황별 마스터클래스 시작! 한국 회사에서 상사에게 칭찬을 받았을 때, 예의 바르면서도 겸손하게 대답하는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Business. Work. Respect. Humility. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host, John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분 안녕하세요! 미나입니다. 오늘부터 리얼 리액션이 새로운 시즌으로 돌아왔어요!
content.translation: Hello everyone! I'm Mina. Starting today, Real Reaction is back with a new season!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이제는 단순히 표현만 배우는 게 아니라 특정 상황에서 어떤 리액션이 가장 완벽한지 알려드릴 거예요.
content.translation: Now, we won't just learn expressions. We'll tell you which reaction is most perfect in specific situations.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I love that. Context is everything! So, what is our first situation today, Mina?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 첫 번째 상황은 바로 한국 회사입니다. 존이 열심히 일해서 부장님께 칭찬을 받은 상황이에요!
content.translation: The first situation is a Korean company. It's a situation where John worked hard and received praise from the director!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh. in that case..., can I just say "대박!?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 절대 안 되죠! 회사에서는 예의와 겸손이 생명이거든요.
content.translation: Absolutely not! In a company, politeness and humility are vital.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 과찬이십니다. This sounds very formal.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그건 "칭찬이 지나치십니다" 라는 뜻이에요. 자신을 낮추고 상대방을 높이는 아주 고급스러운 리액션이에요.
content.translation: Yes. It means "Your praise is excessive." It's a very sophisticated reaction that lowers yourself and raises the other person.

insight.title: 과찬이십니다
insight.description: A formal way to respond to praise
insight.usage_tip: '과찬(Overpraise)' + '이십니다(Is/Formal)'. It's more humble than just saying 'Thank you' in a professional setting.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 한국에서는 칭찬을 받았을 때 덥석 받기보다 한 번쯤 사양하는 게 미덕이거든요.
content.translation: In Korea, it's a virtue to decline at least once rather than just accepting praise right away.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요. 과찬이십니다.
content.translation: No, you're overpraising me.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what about 다 ~덕분입니다.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 "나 혼자 잘해서가 아니라 팀원들과 상사님 덕분이다" 라고 공을 돌리는 표현이에요.
content.translation: This is an expression that shifts the credit, saying "It's not because I did well alone, but thanks to the team members and the boss."

insight.title: 다 ~덕분입니다
insight.usage_tip: '다(All)' + '덕분(Thanks to)' + '입니다(Is)'. It shows you value teamwork and leadership.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이 한마디면 회사에서 "정말 겸손하고 센스 있다" 라는 소리를 들을 수 있어요!
content.translation: With this one word, you can hear that you are "really humble and sensible" at work!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부장님 덕분입니다.
content.translation: It's all thanks to you, Director.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's try. Imagine I'm at the office and my manager is very impressed with my report.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 이번에 작성한 보고서 말이야. 내용이 정말 완벽하던데?
content.translation: Mr. John. regarding the report you wrote this time. The content was truly perfect.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 우리 팀에서 존이 최고야. 아주 수고 많았어!
content.translation: You're the best in our team, John. You did a great job!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요, 부장님. 과찬이십니다.
content.translation: No, Director. You're overpraising me.

insight.title: 아니에요
insight.usage_tip: In this context, it's used to gently decline a compliment as a form of modesty.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니야 정말 잘했어. 다들 존을 좀 본받으라고!
content.translation: No, you really did well. Everyone, follow John's example!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부장님께서 잘 이끌어 주신 덕분입니다.
content.translation: It's all thanks to your great leadership.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 앞으로 더 열심히 하겠습니다!
content.translation: I'll work even harder in the future!

insight.title: 더 열심히 하겠습니다
insight.usage_tip: A standard way to respond to praise while showing future commitment.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 허허. 자네 아주 겸손하구먼. 오늘 회식은 내가 쏘지!
content.translation: Haha. You are very humble. I'll pay for the team dinner today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, humility really works like magic in the Korean workplace!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 칭찬을 받았을 때 겸손하게 반응하면 더 큰 신뢰를 얻을 수 있다는 사실 잊지 마세요.
content.translation: Right. Don't forget that reacting humbly when praised can help you gain even more trust.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 비즈니스 상황에서는 리액션 하나가 내 이미지를 결정한답니다.
content.translation: In a business situation, a single reaction determines your image.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "workplace humble" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 과찬이십니다! You're overpraising me!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 다 덕분입니다! It's all thanks to you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 좋게 봐주셔서 감사합니다! Thank you for seeing it in a good way!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 더 열심히 하겠습니다! I'll work even harder!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부족한 점이 많습니다! I still have a lot to learn!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 누군가에게 칭찬을 듣는다면 겸손하고 따뜻한 리액션으로 화답해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you hear a compliment today, respond with a humble and warm reaction.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 과찬의 말씀을 해주시니 몸 둘 바를 모르겠어요. (I don't know what to do since you're overpraising me.)
친구의 도움 덕분에 일을 무사히 마쳤어요. (I finished the work safely thanks to my friend's help.)
그는 실력이 뛰어나지만 항상 겸손한 태도를 보여요. (He is very skilled but always shows a humble attitude.)
좋은 팀장님께서 저희 팀을 잘 이끌어 주셨어요. (A good team leader led our team well.)
존의 성실한 태도는 우리 모두가 본받아야 해요. (We should all follow John's sincere attitude.)
오늘 프로젝트가 끝나서 다 같이 회식을 하기로 했어요. (Since the project ended today, we decided to have a team dinner together.)
